world.description: A comprehensive world bible for a dark fantasy setting, focusing on corruption, decay, and the terrible costs of power.
sections: Creatures and Dangers of the World

character.description: Detailed profiles of flawed, morally complex protagonists and antagonists, driven by wounds and desperation, with focus on gradual corruption and impossible choices.
focus_areas: Relationship to the Dark Power System

outline.description: A beat-by-beat outline for a dark fantasy novel of 2 chapters (~6400 words), structured around temptation, corruption, and reckoning.
notes: Each chapter should be approximately 3200 words.
The outline must escalate moral stakes with each scene.
Every choice should have a terrible cost.
The climax should question the value of victory achieved through sacrifice.

generation.seed_generate_prompt: Generate a dark fantasy story seed that explores the shadow side of desire, ambition, and survival. The seed should establish a corrupted or dying world where the protagonist faces impossible moral choices. Include: a flawed protagonist desperate enough to make dangerous bargains; a dark power system with seductive appeal and devastating costs; a world where traditional heroism has failed or been twisted; and a central conflict that questions whether salvation is possible or even desirable. Avoid pure evil villains—create antagonists with comprehensible motivations. Emphasize visceral consequences, moral erosion, and the price of power. Output the seed as a single paragraph.
generation.seed_riff_prompt: Given the following story seed: {seed}, expand and deepen it. Add more specific details about the dark power system, the protagonist's personal stakes, the moral compromises they will face, and the oppressive atmosphere of the world. Riff on the seed to produce a richer, more detailed narrative concept that can serve as the foundation for a world bible and character profiles. Ensure the expansion maintains the moral complexity and visceral costs central to dark fantasy.
generation.gen_world_prompt: Using the dark fantasy world concept: {world}, and guided by the following stylistic and tonal voice instructions: {voice_part2}, generate a comprehensive world bible for this setting. The world bible must include the following sections: Corrupted Magic System, History of Catastrophe, Decayed Kingdoms and Factions, Blighted Geography and Sensory Atmosphere, Creatures and Dangers of the World, Societal Structure and Daily Life, Fragments of Beauty and Hope. For each section, provide detailed, sensory-rich descriptions that interlink the magic system, history, geography, factions, and creatures. Emphasize the corruption and decay of the world, how the power system exacts terrible costs, and how every element reinforces the theme of moral erosion and the price of power. Ensure all sections are interconnected and form a coherent, oppressive atmosphere.
generation.gen_characters_prompt: Using the character concepts: {characters}, and the stylistic voice instructions: {voice_part2}, design detailed dark fantasy characters. For each major character, establish: a core wound or desperation that drives them toward dangerous solutions; a moral line they have already crossed or will be forced to cross; relationships based on need, manipulation, or fragile trust; a relationship to the dark power system; a voice that reflects their damage and cynicism; contradictions that show capacity for both cruelty and tenderness. Focus on moral complexity and gradual corruption. Avoid purely sympathetic heroes or irredeemable villains. Make the characters feel real and morally ambiguous.
generation.gen_outline_prompt: Based on the story seed: {seed}, and following the stylistic voice guidelines: {voice_part2}, create a detailed 2-chapter outline for a dark fantasy novel of approximately 6400 words (3200 words per chapter). The outline should follow a three-movement arc: Temptation (the dark power's seductive appeal and first compromises), Corruption (the costs become undeniable, moral erosion accelerates), and Reckoning (the protagonist faces what they have become and the consequences of their choices). For each chapter, provide a chapter overview, key scenes with moral stakes, and the character development beats. Every beat should have clear moral weight and visceral consequences. Ensure the climax questions whether victory is meaningful after the sacrifices made.
generation.gen_outline_part2_prompt: Given the first part of the outline: {part1}, now complete the dark fantasy narrative outline. Generate the second part that escalates the moral costs and leads to a reckoning. The outline should cover the Corruption and Reckoning movements, showing the protagonist's further descent and the terrible price of power. Ensure each scene forces the protagonist into progressively darker choices, and the climax leaves the reader questioning the value of any victory achieved through such compromise.
generation.gen_canon_prompt: From the story seed: {seed}, extract and document the established canon elements for the dark fantasy novel. Focus on the following categories: Magic/Power System Mechanics, World State, Character Wounds and Moral Lines, Relationships and Betrayals, Established Consequences, Thematic Threads, Atmospheric Touchstones, and Prophecies/Oaths/Debts. For each category, list the specific details that must remain consistent throughout the narrative. This canon will guide future development and ensure continuity.
generation.draft_chapter_instructions: Write a dark fantasy chapter of approximately 3200 words, establishing oppressive atmosphere through sensory detail and showing the costs of magic/violence viscerally. The chapter must: (1) immerse the reader in the world through senses (smell, texture, temperature, decay); (2) advance the moral descent of the protagonist, forcing them into choices with consequences; (3) use prose that matches tone—lyrical for corrupted beauty, stark for brutality; (4) create tension through moral stakes, not just physical danger; (5) show the seduction and cost of the dark power system; (6) include moments of humanity that make the darkness more potent by contrast. Ensure continuity with established canon and character voices. Avoid gratuitous darkness—every element should serve theme and character.
generation.anti_pattern_rules: 1. No pure evil villains—give antagonists comprehensible motivations. 2. No unearned hope or easy salvation. 3. Avoid gratuitous violence that does not serve character or theme. 4. Do not let the magic system's costs be merely mentioned—show them through physical, mental, and moral degradation. 5. Do not make protagonist choices arbitrary; each terrible decision must feel necessary in context. 6. Avoid inconsistency in power system rules. 7. Do not neglect sensory atmosphere—the world must feel tangible and oppressive. 8. Avoid melodrama—prose should be controlled and evocative, not overwrought.
generation.arc_summary_premise: In a world blighted by a failed apocalypse, a dishonored knight accepts a cursed blade to protect the last sanctuary of civilization, only to find that the sword whispers its own desires and feeds on the blood of friends and enemies alike. As the knight descends into necessary violence, the line between protector and monster blurs, and the sanctuary's salvation may require a sacrifice beyond measure.
